---
title: "Transformations — Practice Questions"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches

ROOT_CLR  = "#2563EB"
YINT_CLR  = "#16A34A"
ASYM_CLR  = "#DC2626"
CURVE_CLR = "#1e1e1e"
STAT_CLR  = "#9333EA"   # stationary points

def base_ax(ax, xlim=(-5,5), ylim=(-6,6), title="", xlabel="x", ylabel="y"):
    ax.set_xlim(*xlim); ax.set_ylim(*ylim)
    ax.spines[["left","bottom"]].set_position("zero")
    ax.spines[["right","top"]].set_visible(False)
    ax.set_xlabel(xlabel, loc="right", fontsize=9)
    ax.set_ylabel(ylabel, loc="top", rotation=0, fontsize=9)
    ax.set_title(title, fontsize=10, pad=6)
    ax.tick_params(labelsize=7)
    ax.grid(True, linestyle=":", alpha=0.3)

def mark_stat(ax, x, y, label=None, offset=(0.15,0.15)):
    ax.plot(x, y, "o", color=STAT_CLR, ms=7, zorder=5)
    if label:
        ax.annotate(label, (x,y), (x+offset[0], y+offset[1]),
                    fontsize=7.5, color=STAT_CLR,
                    arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", color=STAT_CLR, lw=0.7))

def mark_point(ax, x, y, label=None, color=ROOT_CLR, offset=(0.15,0.15)):
    ax.plot(x, y, "o", color=color, ms=7, zorder=5)
    if label:
        ax.annotate(label, (x,y), (x+offset[0], y+offset[1]),
                    fontsize=7.5, color=color,
                    arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", color=color, lw=0.7))

def asymptote_v(ax, x, label=None):
    ax.axvline(x, color=ASYM_CLR, lw=1.2, ls="--", alpha=0.85)
    if label:
        ax.text(x+0.07, ax.get_ylim()[1]*0.88, label, color=ASYM_CLR, fontsize=7)

def asymptote_h(ax, y, label=None):
    ax.axhline(y, color=ASYM_CLR, lw=1.2, ls="--", alpha=0.85)
    if label:
        ax.text(ax.get_xlim()[0]+0.1, y+0.15, label, color=ASYM_CLR, fontsize=7)

# Define f(x): cubic with max at (-3,2) and min at (2,-4)
# f'(x) = k(x+3)(x-2), solved: k=36/125, C=-236/125
k  = 36/125
C  = -236/125
def f(x):
    return k*(x**3/3 + x**2/2 - 6*x) + C
```

---

## Question 1

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(4.5, 4))
base_ax(ax, xlim=(-5.5,5.5), ylim=(-5.5,4), title="Figure 1")

x = np.linspace(-5.2, 4.5, 600)
ax.plot(x, f(x), color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)

mark_stat(ax, -3, 2,  label="(−3, 2)",  offset=(-1.8, 0.1))
mark_stat(ax,  2, -4, label="(2, −4)",  offset=( 0.2,-0.6))

ax.text(3.5, f(3.5)-0.4, r"$y = f(x)$", fontsize=9)
ax.plot([-5.2],[f(-5.2)]); ax.plot([4.5],[f(4.5)])   # extend curve to edges

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

**Figure 1** shows the curve $y = f(x)$, which has a maximum point at $(-3,\,2)$ and a minimum point at $(2,\,-4)$.

**(a)** Showing the coordinates of any stationary points, sketch on **separate diagrams** the graphs of

> **(i)** $y = f(|x|)$ **(4)**
>
> **(ii)** $y = 3f(2x)$ **(3)**

**(b)** Write down the values of the constants $a$ and $b$ such that the curve $y = a + f(x+b)$ has a minimum point at the **origin** $O$. **(2)**

---

## Question 2

**(a)** Sketch the graph of $y = 2 + \sec\!\left(x - \dfrac{\pi}{6}\right)$ for $x$ in the interval $0 \le x \le 2\pi$.

Show on your sketch the coordinates of any turning points and the equations of any asymptotes. **(5)**

**(b)** Find, in terms of $\pi$, the $x$-coordinates of the points where the graph crosses the $x$-axis. **(5)**

---

---

# Mark Scheme

## Q1 (a)(i) — $y = f(|x|)$ [4 marks]

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(5, 4))
base_ax(ax, xlim=(-5.5,5.5), ylim=(-5.5,4),
        title=r"$y = f(|x|)$  — Mark Scheme")

# Right half (x≥0): same as f(x)
x_right = np.linspace(0, 4.5, 400)
ax.plot(x_right, f(x_right), color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)

# Left half (x<0): mirror of right half, i.e. f(|x|) = f(-x) for x<0
x_left = np.linspace(-4.5, 0, 400)
ax.plot(x_left, f(-x_left), color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)

# Stationary points
mark_stat(ax,  2, -4, label="(2, −4)",   offset=( 0.25,-0.55))
mark_stat(ax, -2, -4, label="(−2, −4)",  offset=(-1.8, -0.55))
mark_stat(ax,  0, f(0), label=f"(0, {f(0):.2f})", offset=(0.2, 0.2))

ax.text(3.2, f(3.2)+0.3, r"$y = f(|x|)$", fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

| Mark | Criteria |
|------|----------|
| M1 | Right half ($x \ge 0$) identical to original $f(x)$ |
| A1 | Minimum at $(2,\,-4)$ correctly labelled |
| M1 | Left half is a **reflection** of the right half in the $y$-axis |
| A1 | Minimum at $(-2,\,-4)$ correctly labelled; original $(-3,\,2)$ max **removed** |

> **Key idea:** $f(|x|)$ replaces the entire left half of $f(x)$ with the mirror image of the right half. The original maximum at $(-3,2)$ disappears.

---

## Q1 (a)(ii) — $y = 3f(2x)$ [3 marks]

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(5, 4))
base_ax(ax, xlim=(-4, 4), ylim=(-14, 9),
        title=r"$y = 3f(2x)$  — Mark Scheme")

x = np.linspace(-4, 3, 600)
ax.plot(x, 3*f(2*x), color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)

# Max: (-3,2) → (-3/2, 6)
mark_stat(ax, -1.5,  6,  label=r"$(-\frac{3}{2},\,6)$",  offset=(-1.5, 0.3))
# Min: (2,-4) → (1, -12)
mark_stat(ax,  1,  -12, label="$(1,\,-12)$", offset=(0.3,-0.8))

ax.text(1.6, 3*f(2*1.6)+0.5, r"$y=3f(2x)$", fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

| Mark | Criteria |
|------|----------|
| M1 | Horizontal stretch by factor $\tfrac{1}{2}$: $x$-coordinates halved |
| A1 | Maximum at $\left(-\tfrac{3}{2},\,6\right)$ correctly labelled |
| A1 | Minimum at $(1,\,-12)$ correctly labelled |

> **Transformation:** $f(x) \to f(2x)$ compresses horizontally by $\tfrac{1}{2}$; then $\times 3$ stretches vertically by $3$.
>
> $$(-3,\,2) \xrightarrow{\div 2 \text{ on }x} \left(-\tfrac{3}{2},\,2\right) \xrightarrow{\times 3 \text{ on }y} \left(-\tfrac{3}{2},\,6\right)$$
> $$(2,\,-4) \xrightarrow{\div 2 \text{ on }x} (1,\,-4) \xrightarrow{\times 3 \text{ on }y} (1,\,-12)$$

---

## Q1 (b) — Values of $a$ and $b$ [2 marks]

**Required:** $y = a + f(x+b)$ has minimum at the origin $(0,\,0)$.

- $f(x+b)$ translates $f(x)$ **left** by $b$. The minimum $(2,\,-4)$ moves to $(2-b,\,-4)$.
- Adding $a$ shifts the graph **up** by $a$. The minimum becomes $(2-b,\,-4+a)$.

Setting this equal to $(0,\,0)$:

$$2 - b = 0 \implies \boxed{b = 2}$$
$$-4 + a = 0 \implies \boxed{a = 4}$$

| Mark | Criteria |
|------|----------|
| B1 | $b = 2$ |
| B1 | $a = 4$ |

---

## Q2 (a) — Sketch of $y = 2 + \sec\!\left(x-\tfrac{\pi}{6}\right)$ [5 marks]

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 4.5))
base_ax(ax, xlim=(-0.2, 2*np.pi+0.2), ylim=(-4, 7),
        title=r"$y = 2 + \sec(x - \pi/6),\quad 0 \leq x \leq 2\pi$  — Mark Scheme")

eps = 0.06
a1 = 2*np.pi/3          # first vertical asymptote
a2 = 5*np.pi/3          # second vertical asymptote

segs = [
    (0,        a1 - eps),
    (a1 + eps, a2 - eps),
    (a2 + eps, 2*np.pi),
]
for lo, hi in segs:
    xs = np.linspace(lo, hi, 500)
    ax.plot(xs, 2 + 1/np.cos(xs - np.pi/6), color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)

# Asymptotes
asymptote_v(ax, a1, label=r"$x=\frac{2\pi}{3}$")
asymptote_v(ax, a2, label=r"$x=\frac{5\pi}{3}$")

# Turning points
mark_stat(ax, np.pi/6,   3,  label=r"$(\frac{\pi}{6},\,3)$",   offset=(0.18, 0.25))
mark_stat(ax, 7*np.pi/6, 1,  label=r"$(\frac{7\pi}{6},\,1)$",  offset=(0.18,-0.55))

# x-intercepts
x1 = 5*np.pi/6; x2 = 3*np.pi/2
mark_point(ax, x1, 0, label=r"$\frac{5\pi}{6}$", color=ROOT_CLR, offset=(0.1, 0.3))
mark_point(ax, x2, 0, label=r"$\frac{3\pi}{2}$", color=ROOT_CLR, offset=(0.1, 0.3))

ax.set_xticks([0, np.pi/6, np.pi/2, 2*np.pi/3, np.pi, 7*np.pi/6,
               5*np.pi/6, 3*np.pi/2, 5*np.pi/3, 2*np.pi])
ax.set_xticklabels(["$0$", r"$\frac{\pi}{6}$", r"$\frac{\pi}{2}$", r"$\frac{2\pi}{3}$",
                    r"$\pi$", r"$\frac{7\pi}{6}$", r"$\frac{5\pi}{6}$",
                    r"$\frac{3\pi}{2}$", r"$\frac{5\pi}{3}$", r"$2\pi$"], fontsize=7)

legend = [
    mpatches.Patch(color=STAT_CLR, label="Turning point"),
    mpatches.Patch(color=ROOT_CLR, label="x-intercept"),
    mpatches.Patch(color=ASYM_CLR, label="Asymptote"),
]
ax.legend(handles=legend, fontsize=7.5, loc="upper right")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

| Mark | Criteria |
|------|----------|
| M1 | Correct shape: two branches of $\sec$ with a minimum-type branch in $[0, \tfrac{2\pi}{3})$ |
| A1 | Vertical asymptotes $x = \tfrac{2\pi}{3}$ and $x = \tfrac{5\pi}{3}$ labelled |
| A1 | Turning point $\left(\tfrac{\pi}{6},\,3\right)$ labelled |
| A1 | Turning point $\left(\tfrac{7\pi}{6},\,1\right)$ labelled |
| A1 | Correct overall shape across all three branches within $[0,2\pi]$ |

**Derivation of key features:**

- **Asymptotes:** $\cos\!\left(x-\tfrac{\pi}{6}\right)=0 \implies x-\tfrac{\pi}{6} = \tfrac{\pi}{2}+n\pi$

  $$x = \tfrac{2\pi}{3} \quad (n=0), \qquad x = \tfrac{5\pi}{3} \quad (n=1)$$

- **Turning points** where $\tan\!\left(x-\tfrac{\pi}{6}\right)=0$, i.e. $x-\tfrac{\pi}{6}=n\pi$:

  $$x=\tfrac{\pi}{6}: \quad y = 2+\sec(0) = 3 \quad \longrightarrow \quad \left(\tfrac{\pi}{6},\,3\right)$$
  $$x=\tfrac{7\pi}{6}: \quad y = 2+\sec(\pi) = 2+(-1) = 1 \quad \longrightarrow \quad \left(\tfrac{7\pi}{6},\,1\right)$$

---

## Q2 (b) — $x$-intercepts [5 marks]

**Set** $y = 0$:

$$2 + \sec\!\left(x-\tfrac{\pi}{6}\right) = 0 \implies \sec\!\left(x-\tfrac{\pi}{6}\right) = -2 \implies \cos\!\left(x-\tfrac{\pi}{6}\right) = -\tfrac{1}{2}$$

Let $\theta = x - \tfrac{\pi}{6}$. Then $\cos\theta = -\tfrac{1}{2}$.

$$\theta = \tfrac{2\pi}{3} \quad \text{or} \quad \theta = \tfrac{4\pi}{3} \qquad (\text{principal values in }[0,2\pi])$$

$$x = \tfrac{2\pi}{3} + \tfrac{\pi}{6} = \tfrac{5\pi}{6} \qquad \text{and} \qquad x = \tfrac{4\pi}{3} + \tfrac{\pi}{6} = \tfrac{3\pi}{2}$$

$$\boxed{x = \dfrac{5\pi}{6} \qquad \text{and} \qquad x = \dfrac{3\pi}{2}}$$

| Mark | Criteria |
|------|----------|
| M1 | Sets $y=0$ and reaches $\sec(\cdots) = -2$ |
| M1 | Converts to $\cos\!\left(x-\tfrac{\pi}{6}\right) = -\tfrac{1}{2}$ |
| A1 | Correct principal value $\theta = \tfrac{2\pi}{3}$ |
| A1 | Second value $\theta = \tfrac{4\pi}{3}$ |
| A1 | Both final answers $x = \tfrac{5\pi}{6}$ and $x = \tfrac{3\pi}{2}$ |
